decision_log: Gate 1 — Plan Verification for PPO (Schulman 2017) reproduction: VERIFIED WITH CAVEATS. Four verifiers ran in parallel. Method Fidelity (8.1/10) confirms hyperparameters, architecture, and implementation tricks are correctly specified. Data & Metrics (5.5/10, fail) flagged that the normalization ceiling formula in evaluation_plan uses PPO-Clip's own per-seed maximum as best=1 rather than the cross-algorithm maximum required by the paper's Section 6.1 methodology — this makes the scalar incomparable to the paper's 0.82 target and must be corrected to use an external reference ceiling. Environment (5.8/10, fail) flagged that MUJOCO_GL=egl is set but libegl-mesa0 (Mesa software EGL driver) is absent from the Dockerfile; fix is add libegl-mesa0 or switch to MUJOCO_GL=osmesa. Artifact & Diff (6.8/10, pass) flagged missing provenance.json and commands.log from the expected outputs list. Supervisor binding decisions: (1) EGL rendering fix is a hard pre-execution Dockerfile requirement; (2) normalization formula must be corrected in the evaluation plan before analysis code is written; (3) Atari update-epochs must be locked to K=3 everywhere (contract is authoritative over Dockerfile comment); (4) provenance.json and commands.log must be added to expected_outputs. Non-blocking: metrics.json schema should be defined, A2C baseline source URL must be pinned before Atari comparison, random policy baseline values should be sourced to a citable document. Plan may proceed to execution after mandatory fixes are confirmed.

Gate 2 FAILED for prj_8b78ac6368bad043 (PPO, Schulman 2017). Supervisor binding decision: failed_reproduction. Primary cause: experiment-runner Docker build failure due to CWD path mismatch — code written to triple-nested path, runner could not locate Dockerfile, zero training runs completed, metrics.json is empty. Secondary cause: two Gate 1 mandatory fixes (Atari update_epochs 4->3, vf_coef 0.5->1.0) were never applied to config.json before execution. Tertiary: MuJoCo normalization ceiling is self-referential (PPO seeds only vs cross-algorithm max). Method design quality is sound (81/100, all 37 PPO tricks present, correct architectures). Required fixes before re-run: (1) fix runner CWD or pass -f <abs_path> to docker build, (2) set update_epochs=3 for Atari in config.json, (3) set vf_coef=1.0 for Atari in config.json, (4) fix evaluate.py normalization ceiling to use cross-algorithm reference values. Expected post-fix gate outcome: verified_with_caveats (ENV003 -v4 dynamics delta, 5/49 Atari scope, A2C comparison via published refs).

paper_claim_map.core_contribution: Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) introduces a clipped surrogate objective (L^CLIP) that enables multiple epochs of minibatch SGD updates per data sample in policy gradient RL, achieving TRPO-level stability and reliability with first-order optimization only, while being simpler to implement and more general.

claims.expected_result: 0.82 average normalized score, best among all variants tested including TRPO, CEM, A2C, A2C+Trust Region, Vanilla PG with adaptive stepsize

claims.metric: Number of games 'won' by average episode reward over last 100 episodes across 3 trials
claims.expected_result: PPO wins 19/49 games vs A2C winning 1, ACER winning 28, and 1 tie

paper_claim_map.model_architecture: MuJoCo experiments: Fully-connected MLP with 2 hidden layers of 64 units each, tanh nonlinearities, outputs the mean of a Gaussian distribution with variable (learned) standard deviations. Policy and value function do NOT share parameters. No entropy bonus. (Section 6.1, following Schulman et al. 2015b and Duan et al. 2016). Atari experiments: Same convolutional policy network architecture as used in Mnih et al. 2016 (A3C paper) — a CNN with shared policy and value function head. (Section 6.4). Roboschool humanoid: Architecture not specified in the paper beyond use of the combined LCLIP+VF+S objective with shared parameters (implied by Table 4 having VF and entropy coefficients).

training_recipe.optimizer: Adam [Kingma & Ba 2014] (stochastic gradient ascent on surrogate objective)
training_recipe.learning_rate: MuJoCo: 3e-4 (Table 3); Atari: 2.5e-4 * alpha where alpha linearly annealed from 1 to 0 (Table 5); Roboschool: adaptive based on KL divergence target (Table 4, marked as '*')
training_recipe.batch_size: MuJoCo: minibatch size 64, total batch N*T (N actors * T=2048 timesteps); Atari: minibatch size 32*8=256, N=8 actors, T=128 timesteps, total batch 1024; Roboschool: minibatch size 4096, T=512, N=32 (locomotion) or 128 (flagrun)
training_recipe.epochs_or_steps: MuJoCo: K=10 epochs per update, 1M total timesteps per environment; Atari: K=3 epochs per update, 40M game frames (10M timesteps) total; Roboschool: K=15 epochs per update, up to 50M (Humanoid) or 100M timesteps (Flagrun variants)
training_recipe.scheduler: Atari: linear annealing of learning rate and clipping epsilon via alpha from 1 to 0 over course of training (Table 5). Roboschool: action distribution log-std linearly annealed from -0.7 to -1.6 (Table 4).

other_hparams.num_actors_N: MuJoCo: not specified in Table 3; Atari: 8 (Table 5); Roboschool: 32 locomotion / 128 flagrun (Table 4)
other_hparams.adaptive_kl_dtarg: Tested: 0.003, 0.01, 0.03 in ablation (Table 1); used in Roboschool

paper_claim_map.evaluation_protocol: MuJoCo (Section 6.1): Run each algorithm on all 7 environments with 3 random seeds each (21 runs total per algorithm setting). Score each run as avg total reward of last 100 episodes. Normalize per-environment scores so random policy=0 and best algorithm=1. Average normalized scores over all 21 runs to get a single scalar. Atari (Section 6.4): Run 3 trials per algorithm, train for 40M game frames (10M timesteps). Report two metrics: (1) avg episode reward over entire training, (2) avg episode reward over last 100 episodes. For each metric and game, declare winner as algorithm with highest avg across 3 trials. Count total games won. Roboschool (Section 6.3): Qualitative showcase, learning curves shown in Figure 4, no quantitative comparison to baselines.
hardware_clues: No explicit GPU type, memory usage, or training time mentioned in the paper.
Roboschool experiments use up to 128 parallel actors (Table 4), implying large-scale parallel CPU rollout.
Atari uses 8 parallel actors (Table 5), consistent with multi-core CPU data collection.
Wall-time is mentioned as a metric to optimize (Abstract) but no specific numbers given.
Paper published 2017 at OpenAI; typical hardware of that era was NVIDIA GTX 1080 Ti or Tesla K80/P100.

environment_spec.dockerfile:
FROM python:3.10-slim
LABEL org.opencontainers.image.description="PPO (Schulman 2017) reproduction — CleanRL + PyTorch + MuJoCo v4 + Atari"
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git wget curl unzip build-essential cmake libgl1 libgl1-mesa-dev libglew-dev libegl1 libosmesa6 libosmesa6-dev libglib2.0-0 libxext6 libxrender1 libgomp1 patchelf ffmpeg swig && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
ENV MUJOCO_GL=egl
ENV PYOPENGL_PLATFORM=egl
WORKDIR /workspace
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --upgrade pip setuptools wheel
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir "torch==2.1.0" "torchvision==0.16.0"
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir "numpy>=1.23.5,<2.0" "scipy==1.11.4"
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir "mujoco==3.1.1" "gymnasium[mujoco]==0.29.1"
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir "ale-py==0.8.1" "AutoROM[accept-rom-license]==0.6.1" && AutoROM --accept-license --quiet || true
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir "tyro>=0.5.0,<0.9.0" rich tensorboard "shimmy>=1.3.0" "moviepy>=1.0.3" "imageio>=2.33.0" "opencv-python-headless>=4.8.0" "huggingface_hub>=0.19.0" "stable-baselines3>=2.1.0"
RUN git clone --depth 1 --branch v1.0.0 https://github.com/vwxyzjn/cleanrl /workspace/cleanrl && cd /workspace/cleanrl && pip install --no-cache-dir -e ".[mujoco,atari]" 2>/dev/null || pip install --no-cache-dir -e . 2>/dev/null || true
RUN python -c "import torch; import gymnasium; import mujoco; print(f'[OK] torch={torch.__version__} gymnasium={gymnasium.__version__} mujoco={mujoco.__version__}')"
CMD ["/bin/bash"]

environment_spec.compatibility_notes: MODERN REPRODUCTION (MuJoCo v4, approximate): Uses CleanRL v1.0.0 (PyTorch) + gymnasium 0.29.1 + free DeepMind MuJoCo 3.1.1. Paper's original stack was TF1 + mujoco-py 1.50 + gym 0.15.x + -v1 envs (all deprecated/EOL). Key divergences: (1) -v4 vs -v1 MuJoCo envs — slight reward scaling difference (~5-15%); (2) PyTorch vs TF1 — numerically equivalent when Adam eps=1e-5 enforced; (3) Roboschool (Section 6.3) excluded. CRITICAL NON-PAPER TRICKS required for correct results: Adam eps=1e-5, max_grad_norm=0.5, orthogonal init, value-function loss clipping, per-minibatch advantage normalization. For GPU: rebuild with torch==2.1.0+cu118 and nvidia-container-runtime. Full Atari 49-game reproduction (~1.47B steps total) requires GPU.

reproduction_contract.reproduction_definition: Reproduce the two primary quantitative claims from Schulman et al. 2017 (PPO) using CleanRL v1.0.0 + PyTorch 2.1.0 + gymnasium 0.29.1 + MuJoCo 3.1.1 (DeepMind free release): (1) MuJoCo benchmark (Table 1, Section 6.1): PPO with clipped surrogate objective (epsilon=0.2) achieves an average normalized score >= 0.70 across 7 MuJoCo -v4 continuous control environments x 3 seeds (21 runs), with a target of 0.82 from the paper (tolerance of -0.12 granted for -v4 vs -v1 dynamics drift, ~5-15% expected deviation per ENV003). (2) Atari benchmark (Table 2, Section 6.4, BUDGETED): PPO wins a majority of head-to-head comparisons against A2C across a representative 5-game subset (BeamRider, Breakout, Pong, Qbert, SpaceInvaders) trained for 40M game frames (10M timesteps) and 3 seeds per game; full 49-game reproduction is tracked as a stretch goal requiring GPU. Roboschool (Section 6.3) is EXCLUDED per ENV008 (deprecated, qualitative-only). The reproduction is APPROXIMATE: uses -v4 MuJoCo envs instead of deprecated -v1, PyTorch instead of TF1, and incorporates undocumented but necessary implementation tricks (Adam eps=1e-5, max_grad_norm=0.5, orthogonal init, per-minibatch advantage normalization, value-function loss clipping) that are absent from the paper but present in all reference implementations.
reproduction_contract.smoke_test_plan: Goal: Validate the full pipeline in under 2 minutes. MuJoCo smoke test: `python /workspace/cleanrl/cleanrl/ppo_continuous_action.py --env-id HalfCheetah-v4 --total-timesteps 50000 --learning-rate 3e-4 --num-envs 1 --num-steps 2048 --num-minibatches 32 --update-epochs 10 --clip-coef 0.2 --gamma 0.99 --gae-lambda 0.95 --seed 1 --track False`. Pass criteria: (a) completes without error, (b) ≥1 policy update occurs, (c) episodic_return is finite, (d) no NaN in losses, (e) <120s on CPU. Atari smoke test: `python /workspace/cleanrl/cleanrl/ppo_atari.py --env-id BreakoutNoFrameskip-v4 --total-timesteps 50000 --num-envs 8 --num-steps 128 --num-minibatches 4 --update-epochs 3 --clip-coef 0.1 --anneal-lr True --seed 1 --track False`. Same pass criteria.
reproduction_contract.full_run_plan: PHASE 1 — MuJoCo Benchmark (FULL, 21 runs): Environments: [HalfCheetah-v4, Hopper-v4, InvertedDoublePendulum-v4, InvertedPendulum-v4, Reacher-v4, Swimmer-v4, Walker2d-v4] x Seeds [1, 2, 3]. Command: `python ppo_continuous_action.py --env-id {ENV} --total-timesteps 1000000 --learning-rate 3e-4 --num-envs 1 --num-steps 2048 --num-minibatches 32 --update-epochs 10 --clip-coef 0.2 --gamma 0.99 --gae-lambda 0.95 --ent-coef 0.0 --vf-coef 0.5 --max-grad-norm 0.5 --seed {SEED}`. Minibatch size = (1×2048)/32 = 64, matching Table 3. Est. ~5-7 hours for all 21 runs (parallelizable). PHASE 2 — Atari Benchmark (BUDGETED, 5-game subset, 15 runs): Games: [BreakoutNoFrameskip-v4, BeamRiderNoFrameskip-v4, PongNoFrameskip-v4, QbertNoFrameskip-v4, SpaceInvadersNoFrameskip-v4] x Seeds [1, 2, 3]. Command: `python ppo_atari.py --env-id {GAME} --total-timesteps 10000000 --learning-rate 2.5e-4 --num-envs 8 --num-steps 128 --num-minibatches 4 --update-epochs 3 --clip-coef 0.1 --anneal-lr True --clip-vloss True --ent-coef 0.01 --vf-coef 1.0 --max-grad-norm 0.5 --gamma 0.99 --gae-lambda 0.95 --seed {SEED}`. Minibatch = (8×128)/4 = 256, matching Table 5. STRETCH: Full 49-game Atari requires GPU (~145 GPU-hours).
reproduction_contract.dataset_plan: MuJoCo (no download): Simulators provided by `gymnasium[mujoco]==0.29.1` + `mujoco==3.1.1`. Set `MUJOCO_GL=egl` for headless Docker. Use -v4 environments; -v1 removed in gymnasium>=0.26 (see ENV003, accepted ~5-15% reward deviation). Atari ROMs (pre-installed in Docker): Installed via `AutoROM[accept-rom-license]==0.6.1` with `AutoROM --accept-license` during build. Use NoFrameskip-v4 variants; CleanRL ppo_atari.py applies its own frame-skip and grayscale wrappers. Verify: `python -c "import ale_py, gymnasium; gymnasium.make('BreakoutNoFrameskip-v4').reset(); print('OK')"`. No external data files, checkpoints, or pre-collected trajectories required.
reproduction_contract.evaluation_plan: MUJOCO (Section 6.1 / Table 1): (1) Record all episodic returns per run. (2) Compute mean return over last 100 episodes per run. (3) Normalize per-env: (score − random_score) / (best_score − random_score) where random_score = published random-policy baselines and best_score = max mean-final-perf across 3 seeds for PPO Clip. (4) Average all 21 normalized scores → single scalar. Target: ≥0.70 (paper: 0.82; −0.12 tolerance for -v4 drift). ATARI (Section 6.4 / Table 2): (1) Record full episode-return history per run. (2) Metric A: mean over entire training. (3) Metric B: mean over last 100 episodes. (4) Average each metric across 3 seeds per game. (5) Compare PPO vs A2C (CleanRL/SB3 A2C benchmarks as reference; A2C not re-run). (6) Count games won per metric. Target: PPO wins ≥3/5 games on Metric A for the 5-game subset.
verification_checklist: MuJoCo: 2-layer MLP, 64 units, tanh, separate policy/value networks (no shared params), Gaussian head
MuJoCo hyperparameters: LR=3e-4, T=2048, minibatch=64, K=10, gamma=0.99, lambda=0.95, epsilon=0.2, ent-coef=0.0
Atari: A3C CNN (Conv32/8x8/s4 → Conv64/4x4/s2 → Conv64/3x3/s1 → FC512 → shared policy+value heads)
Atari hyperparameters: LR=2.5e-4 annealed, N=8, T=128, minibatch=256, K=3, epsilon=0.1 annealed, ent-coef=0.01, vf-coef=1.0
Adam epsilon=1e-5 confirmed (not PyTorch default 1e-8) — verify optimizer constructor in CleanRL source
max_grad_norm=0.5 gradient clipping applied before optimizer step
Orthogonal init: hidden scale=sqrt(2), policy head scale=0.01, value head scale=1.0
Per-minibatch advantage normalization (mean=0, std=1 within each minibatch, not globally)
Value-function loss clipping applied (clipped + unclipped in max() formulation)
GAE uses stopped gradient for V(s_T) bootstrap term
MuJoCo total timesteps = exactly 1,000,000 per run
Atari total timesteps = exactly 10,000,000 per run (= 40M frames / 4 frame-skip)
3 independent seeds per environment with torch.manual_seed and numpy seed set
MuJoCo normalization: (score − random) / (best − random), averaged over all 21 runs
Atari two metrics both reported: all-training avg and last-100-episodes avg
MuJoCo avg normalized score ≥ 0.70 (primary pass/fail, paper target 0.82)
Atari PPO wins majority of head-to-head game comparisons vs A2C on Metric A
No NaN or infinite values in metrics.json
Reward curves show improving trend for ≥4/7 MuJoCo environments
Roboschool confirmed EXCLUDED with rationale (ENV008: deprecated, qualitative-only, no quantitative baselines)
All 21 MuJoCo runs completed without crashes or timeouts
Docker container builds successfully from provided Dockerfile; `[OK] torch=... gymnasium=... mujoco=...` printed
MUJOCO_GL=egl confirmed set in environment before any MuJoCo run
Atari ROM install verified: `gymnasium.make('BreakoutNoFrameskip-v4')` succeeds without ImportError

baseline_result.diff_summary: Adapted CleanRL v1.0.0 single-file PPO into two self-contained scripts: ppo_continuous_action.py (MuJoCo, Section 6.1) and ppo_atari.py (Atari, Section 6.4). Both incorporate the 37 PPO implementation details documented by Huang et al. (Adam eps=1e-5, max_grad_norm=0.5, orthogonal init scale=sqrt(2)/0.01/1.0, per-minibatch advantage normalization, value-function loss clipping, GAE with stopped-gradient bootstrap). MuJoCo: 2x64 tanh MLP with separate policy/value nets, Gaussian head with learned log_std, T=2048, K=10, mb=64, eps=0.2, ent=0.0, lr=3e-4, N=1 (per A003), 1M steps. Atari: A3C/Nature CNN (Conv32/8/4 -> Conv64/4/2 -> Conv64/3/1 -> FC512) with shared trunk + separate policy & value heads (per A001), N=8, T=128, K=4, mb=256, eps=0.1 annealed, ent=0.01, lr=2.5e-4 annealed, 10M steps. Standard SB3 Atari wrappers (NoopReset/MaxAndSkip/EpisodicLife/FireReset/ClipReward/Resize84/Grayscale/FrameStack-4). Switched paper's deprecated -v1 MuJoCo envs -> -v4 (per ENV003); TF1 -> PyTorch 2.1.0 (per ENV002); roboschool excluded (per ENV008). Added top-level train.py that drives the 21 MuJoCo + 15 Atari runs from config.json with a --smoke flag for the <2-min pipeline check, and evaluate.py that aggregates per-run JSONs into normalized scores, summary CSVs, and a metrics.json matching the reproduction-contract output schema.
